controls.Title: Legacy Authentication Blocked
controls.Description: A Conditional Access policy blocks legacy authentication protocols. Legacy auth bypasses MFA entirely.
controls.BusinessRisk: Any account using legacy auth can be compromised without MFA. Attackers actively spray legacy auth endpoints because MFA cannot intercept them.
controls.Remediation: Create a CA policy: All Users, Client apps = Other clients + Exchange ActiveSync, Grant = Block access.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: MFA Required for All Users
controls.Description: A Conditional Access policy or Security Defaults requires MFA for all users on every sign-in.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without MFA, a single stolen password enables full account takeover. Password spray, phishing, and credential stuffing all succeed.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Users = All, Apps = All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA. Or enable Security Defaults.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Phishing-Resistant MFA Required for Admins
controls.Description: CA policy requires phishing-resistant MFA (Authentication Strength: FIDO2, CBA, or Windows Hello) for all privileged directory roles.
controls.BusinessRisk: Standard MFA is bypassed by AiTM proxy phishing attacks. Attackers use Evilginx2 to steal session tokens even when MFA is completed.
controls.Remediation: CA policy targeting privileged roles with Authentication Strength = Phishing-resistant MFA. Must cover Global Administrator at minimum.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Conditional Access Policies Deployed
controls.Description: At minimum three enabled CA policies provide coverage for legacy auth block, MFA all users, and MFA for admins.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without CA, access control is password-only. Security Defaults is a fallback — not a substitute for a mature CA posture.
controls.Remediation: Deploy at minimum: (1) block legacy auth, (2) require MFA all users, (3) phishing-resistant MFA for admin roles.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Global Administrator Count 2-8, Cloud-Only
controls.Description: The tenant has 2-8 Global Administrators, all cloud-only (not synced from on-premises AD).
controls.BusinessRisk: More than 8 GAs increases attack surface. Synced GA accounts allow on-prem compromise to escalate directly to cloud tenant control.
controls.Remediation: Reduce GA count to 2-8 break-glass accounts. Remove GA from any on-premises synced accounts and assign to cloud-only accounts.

controls.Title: Mailbox Audit Logging Enabled
controls.Description: Organization-level mailbox audit logging is enabled and not disabled at the tenant or per-mailbox level.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without mailbox auditing, there is no record of who accessed, sent, or deleted emails. BEC and data exfiltration investigation is impossible.
controls.Remediation: Set-OrganizationConfig -AuditDisabled $false

controls.Title: SMTP Client Authentication Disabled
controls.Description: SMTP AUTH is disabled at the tenant level with no per-mailbox exceptions.
controls.BusinessRisk: SMTP AUTH accepts passwords directly. Compromised accounts with SMTP AUTH enabled can send email without MFA, enabling BEC campaigns.
controls.Remediation: Set-TransportConfig -SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled $true

controls.Title: External Auto-Forwarding Blocked
controls.Description: Outbound spam policy blocks automatic forwarding to external recipients. Remote domain wildcard has AutoForwardEnabled = false.
controls.BusinessRisk: Compromised accounts set forwarding rules to exfiltrate all email to attacker addresses. Without this control, BEC exfiltration runs indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedOutboundSpamFilterPolicy -Identity Default -AutoForwardingMode Off. Set-RemoteDomain * -AutoForwardEnabled $false

controls.Title: DKIM Signing Enabled for All Domains
controls.Description: DKIM signing is enabled for all accepted domains with 2048-bit keys on selector1 and selector2.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without DKIM, email cannot be cryptographically verified and messages can be modified in transit without detection.
controls.Remediation: Enable-DkimSigningConfig -Identity domain.com. Publish the CNAME records from M365 admin center.

controls.Title: Anti-Phishing Impersonation Protection Enabled
controls.Description: Default anti-phishing policy has user impersonation, organization domain protection, mailbox intelligence, and external sender tagging all enabled.
controls.BusinessRisk: Impersonation attacks are the primary BEC vector. Without protection, lookalike domains and display name spoofs reach inboxes undetected.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -EnableTargetedUserProtection $true -EnableOrganizationDomainsProtection $true -EnableMailboxIntelligence $true -EnableExternalSenderTag $true

controls.Title: Modern Authentication Enabled
controls.Description: Modern authentication (OAuth2) is enabled for Exchange Online.
controls.BusinessRisk: Disabling modern auth forces basic authentication, making MFA ineffective for email clients and bypassing Conditional Access.
controls.Remediation: Set-OrganizationConfig -OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled $true

controls.Title: Honor DMARC Policy Enabled
controls.Description: Anti-phishing policy honors the DMARC policy of sending domains, applying reject or quarantine as specified.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without this setting, EXO ignores p=reject DMARC records from legitimate senders, accepting spoofed email from well-protected domains.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -HonorDmarcPolicy $true

controls.Title: SPF Record Published
controls.Description: All sending domains have a valid SPF record ending in -all or ~all.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without SPF, anyone can send email claiming to be from the domain. SPF is required for DMARC alignment.
controls.Remediation: Publish: v=spf1 include:spf.protection.outlook.com -all. Add include: for all third-party senders before the -all qualifier.

controls.Title: DKIM Records Published
controls.Description: DKIM CNAME records are published for selector1 and selector2 for all sending domains.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without DKIM, DMARC alignment fails. DKIM is the only authentication mechanism that survives email forwarding.
controls.Remediation: Enable DKIM in M365 admin center for each domain. Publish the two CNAME records provided.

controls.Title: DMARC Policy at Quarantine or Reject
controls.Description: All sending domains have DMARC with p=quarantine or p=reject at 100% coverage. p=none provides zero protection.
controls.BusinessRisk: p=none is monitoring-only. Attackers can spoof the exact From: address with no delivery interference.
controls.Remediation: Start with p=none and rua= reporting. Advance to p=quarantine then p=reject as all legitimate senders pass alignment. Target: v=DMARC1; p=reject; pct=100; rua=mailto:dmarc@domain.com

controls.Title: MTA-STS Policy in Enforce Mode
controls.Description: MTA-STS DNS record and HTTPS policy file published with mode: enforce.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without MTA-STS, SMTP connections can be TLS-downgraded by on-path attackers, exposing email in transit.
controls.Remediation: Publish _mta-sts TXT record and host policy file at https://mta-sts.domain.com/.well-known/mta-sts.txt with mode: enforce.

controls.Title: TLS-RPT Record Published
controls.Description: TLS-RPT DNS record is published to receive reports of TLS failures on inbound SMTP delivery. (No direct SCuBA mapping — DNS/email-auth controls handled out-of-band.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without TLS-RPT, TLS failures and downgrade attempts go undetected. Required for MTA-STS monitoring.
controls.Remediation: Publish: _smtp._tls.domain.com TXT 'v=TLSRPTv1; rua=mailto:tlsrpt@domain.com'

controls.Title: DNSSEC Enabled
controls.Description: DNSSEC is enabled with DS records published at the registrar. (No direct SCuBA mapping — DNS/email-auth controls handled out-of-band.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without DNSSEC, DNS responses can be forged by cache poisoning, redirecting email and undermining SPF/DKIM/DMARC.
controls.Remediation: Enable DNSSEC at your registrar. GoDaddy: Domains > DNS > DNSSEC > Enable. Free for up to 5 domains.

controls.Title: DKIM Key Rotation Cadence
controls.Description: DKIM signing keys are rotated on a documented cadence (target: <= 365 days) for each sending domain.
controls.BusinessRisk: Long-lived DKIM keys broaden the impact of a key-compromise event and weaken DMARC reliance on DKIM signatures. Microsoft does not auto-rotate customer-managed DKIM keys, so many tenants run keys older than two years.
controls.Remediation: Rotate DKIM keys: Set-DkimSigningConfig -Identity domain.com -KeyCreationTime (Get-Date) -BodyCanonicalization Relaxed -HeaderCanonicalization Relaxed. Plan a 12-month cadence and document rotation in the change record.

controls.Title: CAA Record Restricts Certificate Issuance
controls.Description: DNS Certification Authority Authorization (CAA) records (RFC 8659) name the approved CAs for the domain.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without CAA, any publicly trusted CA may issue certificates for the domain. A phishing-driven approval at a non-approved CA has no DNS-level brake, enabling on-path TLS interception or impersonation.
controls.Remediation: Publish CAA records at the registrar naming approved CAs. Example: 'domain.com. IN CAA 0 issue "digicert.com"' and 'domain.com. IN CAA 0 issuewild ";"' to deny wildcard issuance. Include 'iodef' contact email for mis-issuance reports.

controls.Title: TLS Certificate Expiry on Mail Hostnames
controls.Description: TLS certificates on autodiscover and mail hostnames have at least 90 days of remaining validity.
controls.BusinessRisk: Expired TLS certs break autodiscover, Outlook on the web, and mail flow. Last-minute renewals invite errors and rush approvals at unauthorized CAs.
controls.Remediation: Renew TLS certs at least 30 days before expiry. Automate renewal via ACME (Let's Encrypt) or your registrar's managed cert service. Alert when DaysUntilExpiry drops below 30.

controls.Title: Certificate Transparency Log Hygiene
controls.Description: All certs issued for the domain (visible in CT logs per RFC 6962) originate from CAs on the org-approved allowlist.
controls.BusinessRisk: A cert issued by an unapproved CA in CT logs may indicate domain takeover, social-engineered cert approval, or use of an unsanctioned third-party service that proxies the domain.
controls.Remediation: Review crt.sh entries for the domain. Confirm each unrecognized issuer corresponds to an approved third-party (CDN, SaaS subdomain). Add the approved CA to CAA. Subscribe to CT log monitoring (e.g., Cert Spotter) for ongoing mis-issuance detection.

controls.Title: Safe Attachments Enabled with Block Action
controls.Description: Defender for Office 365 Safe Attachments policies are enabled with action = Block.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without Safe Attachments, weaponized Office docs and PDFs with exploits are delivered to inboxes without sandbox analysis.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Policies > Safe Attachments > Create policy for all recipients with Action = Block. Requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 1.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 (M365 Business Premium)

controls.Title: Safe Links Enabled and Hardened
controls.Description: Safe Links enabled for email with AllowClickThrough=$false, TrackClicks=$true, EnableForInternalSenders=$true.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without Safe Links, time-of-click URL swapping bypasses pre-delivery scanning.
controls.Remediation: Create Safe Links policy with AllowClickThrough=$false, TrackClicks=$true, EnableForInternalSenders=$true. Requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 1.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 (M365 Business Premium)

controls.Title: Spoof Intelligence Enabled
controls.Description: Anti-phishing policy has spoof intelligence enabled.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without spoof intelligence, spoofed senders that fail SPF/DKIM pass without evaluation.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -EnableSpoofIntelligence $true

controls.Title: Honor DMARC Policy (Defender Layer)
controls.Description: Anti-phishing policy honors DMARC policy of sending domains.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without this, EXO delivers email from p=reject domains, undermining the global DMARC ecosystem.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -HonorDmarcPolicy $true

controls.Title: Phishing Threshold Level Aggressive
controls.Description: Anti-phishing PhishThresholdLevel is set to 2 (Aggressive) or higher.
controls.BusinessRisk: Standard threshold misses sophisticated phishing that aggressive scanning catches.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -PhishThresholdLevel 2

controls.Title: First Contact Safety Tip Enabled
controls.Description: Anti-phishing shows a safety tip when users receive email from a first-time sender.
controls.BusinessRisk: First contact attacks rely on users not recognizing unfamiliar senders.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -EnableFirstContactSafetyTips $true

controls.Title: External Sharing Restricted
controls.Description: SharePoint external sharing is set to Existing Guests or less — not Anyone (anonymous links).
controls.BusinessRisk: Anonymous sharing links can be accessed by anyone with the URL, with no authentication or audit trail.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -SharingCapability ExistingExternalUserSharingOnly

controls.Title: Default Sharing Link Not Anonymous
controls.Description: Default sharing link type is Internal or Specific people — not Anyone.
controls.BusinessRisk: Default anonymous links means users create them accidentally without understanding the exposure.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -DefaultSharingLinkType Internal

controls.Title: SharePoint Legacy Authentication Blocked
controls.Description: SharePoint blocks legacy authentication, requiring modern auth for all access.
controls.BusinessRisk: Legacy auth to SharePoint bypasses MFA and CA, allowing password-only access to documents.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -LegacyAuthProtocolsEnabled $false

controls.Title: Guest Access Expiration Configured
controls.Description: Guest access to SharePoint is configured to expire and require re-authentication.
controls.BusinessRisk: Permanent guest access means departed vendors and compromised external accounts retain access indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -ExternalUserExpirationRequired $true -ExternalUserExpireInDays 60

controls.Title: Unmanaged Device Access Restricted
controls.Description: SharePoint access from unmanaged devices is limited to browser-only or blocked.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unmanaged devices have no endpoint controls. Data downloaded leaves the organization's security boundary.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -ConditionalAccessPolicy AllowLimitedAccess

controls.Title: External Federation Access Restricted
controls.Description: Teams federation is not open to all domains.
controls.BusinessRisk: Open federation allows any Teams user on any tenant to contact your users directly, enabling large-scale social engineering.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTenantFederationConfiguration -AllowFederatedUsers $false, or restrict to an allowlist of trusted domains.

controls.Title: Teams Consumer Account Access Disabled
controls.Description: Personal Microsoft accounts (Teams consumer) cannot contact or join meetings in the tenant.
controls.BusinessRisk: Consumer accounts are unverifiable. Allowing them enables social engineering from identities that cannot be audited or revoked.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTenantFederationConfiguration -AllowTeamsConsumer $false -AllowTeamsConsumerInbound $false

controls.Title: Anonymous Meeting Join Disabled
controls.Description: Anonymous users cannot join Teams meetings without admission by an authenticated participant.
controls.BusinessRisk: Anonymous join allows unauthenticated individuals to eavesdrop on internal meetings via leaked meeting links.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowAnonymousUsersToJoinMeeting $false

controls.Title: External Participants Cannot Take Screen Control
controls.Description: Meeting policy prevents external participants from taking or requesting screen share control.
controls.BusinessRisk: Screen control transfer to external participants can expose sensitive on-screen data.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowExternalParticipantGiveRequestControl $false

controls.Title: Recording Storage in Organization
controls.Description: Meeting recordings are stored in SharePoint/OneDrive with organization retention policies applied.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without defined storage and retention, recordings containing sensitive discussions may be retained indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: Ensure Purview retention policies cover SharePoint to capture Teams recording data.

controls.Title: Teams Guest Access Controlled
controls.Description: Teams guest access is disabled or limited to verified external collaborators with controlled invitation permissions.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unrestricted guest access allows any user to invite external parties to internal Teams and files without IT oversight.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsClientConfiguration -AllowGuestUser $false, or configure guest invitation permissions in Entra External Identities.

controls.Title: Unified Audit Log Enabled
controls.Description: Microsoft Purview unified audit logging is enabled across M365 services. (No SCuBA equivalent — Purview audit/DLP covered in MS.DEFENDER.* baselines.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without audit logging, there is no record of admin changes, data access, or security policy modifications. Incident investigation is impossible.
controls.Remediation: Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled $true

controls.Title: Audit Log Retention Minimum 90 Days
controls.Description: Unified audit log retention is configured for at least 90 days.
controls.BusinessRisk: Short retention prevents investigation of incidents discovered after the retention window closes.
controls.Remediation: Configure custom retention policies in Purview Compliance > Audit > Retention policies. E5 provides 1-year default.

controls.Title: DLP Policy Active for Sensitive Data
controls.Description: At least one active DLP policy protects sensitive information types from unauthorized sharing. (No SCuBA equivalent — Purview audit/DLP covered in MS.DEFENDER.* baselines.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without DLP, PII, financial data, and PHI can be emailed externally or shared with unauthenticated recipients without detection.
controls.Remediation: Create DLP policies in Purview > Data loss prevention using Microsoft-provided templates (Financial, PII, Health) across Exchange, SharePoint, Teams.

controls.Title: Sensitivity Labels Published
controls.Description: At least one sensitivity label policy is published to users.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without classification labels, sensitive data cannot be automatically protected or audited.
controls.Remediation: Create labels in Purview > Information protection. Publish via label policies. Minimum tiers: Internal, Confidential, Highly Confidential.

controls.Title: Device Compliance Policies Configured
controls.Description: Intune compliance policies are configured and evaluating enrolled devices against security baselines. (No SCuBA equivalent — Intune device-compliance controls are out of SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without compliance policies, enrolled devices have no enforced security requirements. Non-compliant devices access corporate resources unchecked.
controls.Remediation: Create compliance policies per platform in Intune > Devices > Compliance. Require encryption, password, and OS version minimums.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Non-Compliant Device Access Blocked via CA
controls.Description: CA policies block or limit M365 access from devices marked non-compliant by Intune. (No SCuBA equivalent — Intune device-compliance controls are out of SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Compliance policies without CA enforcement are informational only. Non-compliant devices continue accessing data.
controls.Remediation: Create CA policy: require compliant device or hybrid join for all cloud apps.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: BitLocker Encryption Required on Windows
controls.Description: Windows compliance policy requires BitLocker disk encryption. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Unencrypted laptops allow physical theft to result in data breach.
controls.Remediation: Intune compliance policy for Windows: Device Health > Require BitLocker = Require.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Mobile Application Management Configured
controls.Description: Intune app protection policies enforce data containment on iOS and Android. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: BYOD without MAM can copy corporate data from managed apps to personal apps with no controls.
controls.Remediation: Create app protection policies in Intune > Apps > App protection policies for iOS and Android targeting Office apps.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Antivirus Policy Deployed via Intune
controls.Description: Intune endpoint security antivirus policies are deployed to Windows devices, enforcing Defender AV settings. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without enforced AV policy, users can disable Defender or create exclusions, leaving endpoints unprotected.
controls.Remediation: Create Endpoint Security > Antivirus policy in Intune. Set CloudBlockLevel = High, enable real-time protection and tamper protection.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Power Platform Tenant Isolation Enabled
controls.Description: Power Platform tenant isolation restricts cross-tenant connector connections.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without isolation, Power Automate flows can connect to external tenant resources, enabling data exfiltration via legitimate connectors.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Policies > Tenant Isolation: enable and configure inbound/outbound restrictions.

controls.Title: Power Platform DLP Policy Active
controls.Description: A DLP policy for Power Platform classifies connectors as Business, Non-Business, or Blocked.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without DLP, flows can move data between any connectors including personal cloud storage and social media.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Policies > Data policies: create environment policy. Move business connectors to Business group, block consumer connectors.

controls.Title: Power Platform Environment Creation Restricted
controls.Description: Non-admin users cannot create new Power Platform environments.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unrestricted environment creation allows data exfiltration through ungoverned flows outside DLP policy scope.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Settings: restrict environment creation to admins only.

controls.Title: POP3 Access Disabled
controls.Description: POP3 is disabled at the tenant level. POP3 uses basic authentication and cannot be protected by MFA.
controls.BusinessRisk: POP3 allows credential-based email access that bypasses MFA and Conditional Access.
controls.Remediation: Set-TransportConfig -PopEnabled $false. Per-mailbox: Get-CASMailbox -ResultSize Unlimited | Set-CASMailbox -PopEnabled $false

controls.Title: IMAP Access Disabled
controls.Description: IMAP is disabled at the tenant level. IMAP uses basic authentication and cannot be protected by MFA.
controls.BusinessRisk: IMAP allows credential-based email access bypassing MFA and CA policies.
controls.Remediation: Set-TransportConfig -ImapEnabled $false. Per-mailbox: Get-CASMailbox -ResultSize Unlimited | Set-CASMailbox -ImapEnabled $false

controls.Title: Customer Lockbox Enabled
controls.Description: Customer Lockbox requires explicit admin approval before Microsoft support can access tenant data.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without Customer Lockbox, Microsoft support can access tenant data without notification or approval during support cases.
controls.Remediation: Enable in M365 admin center > Settings > Security & privacy > Customer Lockbox. Requires E5 or E5 Compliance add-on.

controls.Title: Shared Mailboxes Block Direct Sign-In
controls.Description: Shared mailbox accounts are blocked from direct sign-in (no active user account associated).
controls.BusinessRisk: Shared mailboxes with active accounts can be signed into directly with a password, bypassing MFA. Attackers target shared mailboxes for persistence.
controls.Remediation: Get-Mailbox -RecipientTypeDetails SharedMailbox | ForEach-Object { Set-MgUser -UserId $_.ExternalDirectoryObjectId -AccountEnabled $false }

controls.Title: Sign-in Risk CA Policy
controls.Description: CA policy enforces MFA or blocks access when sign-in risk is elevated. Requires Entra ID P2.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without sign-in risk policies, compromised credentials and impossible travel go unchallenged.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Condition = Sign-in risk high/medium, Grant = Require MFA or Block. Requires Entra P2.

controls.Title: User Risk CA Policy
controls.Description: CA policy enforces password change or blocks access when user risk is elevated.
controls.BusinessRisk: High user risk indicates credential compromise. Without automated response, compromised accounts remain active.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Condition = User risk high, Grant = Require password change. Requires Entra P2.

controls.Title: Named Locations Defined
controls.Description: At least one trusted named location (IP range) is defined in Entra ID for use in CA policies. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without named locations, CA cannot distinguish access from trusted office networks vs untrusted locations.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Security > Named locations > Add IP ranges location. Mark office and VPN IP ranges as trusted.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Device Compliance Enforced via CA
controls.Description: CA policy requires compliant or hybrid-joined device for access to M365 resources.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without device compliance enforcement, unmanaged personal devices access corporate data with no endpoint controls.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Grant = Require device to be marked as compliant, or Require hybrid Azure AD joined device.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1 + Intune

controls.Title: No Permanent Admin Role Assignments
controls.Description: Privileged roles use PIM eligible assignments — no permanent active assignments outside break-glass accounts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Permanent admin accounts are always high-value targets. PIM limits the window of privilege exposure to when it is actually needed.
controls.Remediation: Convert permanent role assignments to PIM eligible. Keep only break-glass accounts as permanent GA.

controls.Title: PIM Requires MFA on Activation
controls.Description: PIM role management policies require MFA when users activate an eligible role assignment.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without MFA on activation, a compromised account can silently elevate to Global Admin via PIM with just a password.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Roles > Select role > Settings > Require MFA on activation.

controls.Title: PIM Requires Justification on Activation
controls.Description: PIM requires users to provide a reason when activating an eligible role assignment.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without justification, privileged access activations have no audit trail. Insider threat and accidental escalation go undocumented.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Roles > Select role > Settings > Require justification on activation.

controls.Title: PIM GA Activation Requires Approval
controls.Description: Global Administrator PIM activation requires approval from a designated reviewer.
controls.BusinessRisk: Self-approval GA elevation means any compromised eligible account reaches global admin without a second control point.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Global Administrator > Settings > Require approval > Add approvers.

controls.Title: PIM Max Activation Duration 8 Hours or Less
controls.Description: PIM role policies set maximum activation duration to 8 hours or less.
controls.BusinessRisk: Longer activation windows increase the time a compromised activation session can be abused.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Roles > Select role > Settings > Maximum activation duration = 8 hours.

controls.Title: Guest Invite Permissions Restricted
controls.Description: Only admins or Guest Inviter role members can invite external guest users.
controls.BusinessRisk: Any member being able to invite guests enables uncontrolled external access provisioning without IT oversight.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings > Guest invite settings = Admins and users in the Guest Inviter role.

controls.Title: External Collaboration Settings Restricted
controls.Description: Users cannot create new tenants and legacy MSOL PowerShell is blocked.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unrestricted external collaboration settings enable shadow IT tenant creation and legacy protocol abuse.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > User settings > Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants. Block MSOL PowerShell via authorization policy.

controls.Title: B2B Guest Default Permissions Restricted
controls.Description: Guest users are assigned the Restricted Guest User role — not member-equivalent permissions.
controls.BusinessRisk: Guest users with member-level permissions can enumerate users, groups, and applications — gathering intelligence for targeted attacks.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings > Guest user access = Most restricted.

controls.Title: Self-Service Password Reset Enabled
controls.Description: SSPR is enabled allowing users to reset their own passwords without helpdesk involvement. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without SSPR, helpdesk becomes a social engineering target — attackers call claiming to be users to get password resets.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Password reset > Properties > Self service password reset enabled = All or Selected.

controls.Title: SSPR Requires Multiple Authentication Methods
controls.Description: SSPR is configured to require at least two authentication methods for password reset. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Single-method SSPR allows account takeover via one compromised recovery method (phone SIM swap, email compromise).
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Password reset > Authentication methods > Number of methods required = 2.

controls.Title: User App Registration Disabled
controls.Description: Non-admin users cannot register new applications in Entra ID.
controls.BusinessRisk: Users registering apps can accidentally or deliberately grant excessive API permissions, creating OAuth attack surfaces.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > User settings > App registrations > Users can register applications = No.

controls.Title: User Consent to Apps Restricted
controls.Description: Users cannot consent to application permissions — all consent goes through admin workflow.
controls.BusinessRisk: Consent phishing grants attacker-controlled apps access to mailbox and files. Users cannot distinguish malicious OAuth consent from legitimate.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Enterprise apps > Consent and permissions > User consent settings = Do not allow user consent.

controls.Title: Admin Consent Workflow Enabled
controls.Description: Admin consent workflow allows users to request app access that goes through an approval process.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without a consent workflow, users with blocked consent have no approved path — they use shadow IT alternatives instead.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Enterprise apps > Consent and permissions > Admin consent requests > Yes + configure reviewers.

controls.Title: Password Protection Lockout Configured
controls.Description: Account lockout threshold is configured at 10 or fewer failed attempts. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: High lockout thresholds allow extensive brute force attempts before accounts are locked.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Security > Authentication methods > Password protection > Lockout threshold ≤10.

controls.Title: Break-Glass Accounts Configured
controls.Description: At least two cloud-only GA accounts are excluded from CA policies as emergency access accounts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without break-glass accounts, a CA policy misconfiguration can lock all admins out of the tenant permanently.
controls.Remediation: Create two cloud-only GA accounts with strong random passwords, exclude from all CA policies, store credentials in secure offline location. Monitor for sign-in activity.

controls.Title: Preset Security Policies Applied
controls.Description: Microsoft Standard or Strict preset security policy is active, providing baseline Defender configuration.
controls.BusinessRisk: Custom-only configurations may miss settings covered by preset policies. Presets provide Microsoft-recommended defaults as a starting floor.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Email & Collaboration > Policies > Preset security policies > Apply Standard or Strict protection.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 (M365 Business Premium)

controls.Title: Zero-Hour Auto Purge Enabled
controls.Description: ZAP retroactively removes malicious mail from user mailboxes after delivery when new threat intelligence identifies it.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without ZAP, malware or phishing delivered before a signature update is never cleaned up from inboxes.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedContentFilterPolicy -ZapEnabled $true

controls.Title: Anti-Malware Common Attachments Blocked
controls.Description: Common attachment type filter blocks high-risk file extensions regardless of content scan result.
controls.BusinessRisk: Signature-based scanning can be evaded. Blocking .exe, .js, .vbs, .ps1 by extension provides defense-in-depth.
controls.Remediation: Set-MalwareFilterPolicy -Default -EnableFileFilter $true

controls.Title: Phishing Directed to Quarantine Not Junk
controls.Description: Phishing spam action is Quarantine — not MoveToJmf (junk folder). Quarantined mail is admin-managed.
controls.BusinessRisk: Phishing in junk folders can still be accessed and clicked by users. Quarantine prevents all interaction.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedContentFilterPolicy -PhishSpamAction Quarantine

controls.Title: High Confidence Spam to Quarantine
controls.Description: High confidence spam action is Quarantine.
controls.BusinessRisk: High-confidence spam in junk remains accessible. Quarantine requires admin review before release.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedContentFilterPolicy -HighConfidenceSpamAction Quarantine

controls.Title: Bulk Mail Threshold Configured
controls.Description: Bulk complaint level threshold is set to 6 or lower for improved bulk mail filtering.
controls.BusinessRisk: Default threshold (7) allows significant bulk/graymail through. Lower values reduce phishing that hides in bulk campaigns.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedContentFilterPolicy -BulkThreshold 6

controls.Title: Connection Filter Safe List Bypass Disabled
controls.Description: Microsoft safe list bypass is disabled on the default connection filter policy. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Safe list bypass allows senders on Microsoft-maintained lists to skip all EOP filtering — third-party senders can exploit this.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedConnectionFilterPolicy -Identity Default -EnableSafeList $false

controls.Title: Outbound Spam Sending Limits Configured
controls.Description: Outbound spam policy is configured with appropriate per-user sending limits and admin notification. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Compromised accounts can send mass phishing without rate limiting. Limits and alerts provide early BEC detection.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedOutboundSpamFilterPolicy: configure RecipientLimitInternalPerHour, RecipientLimitExternalPerHour, ActionWhenThresholdReached = Alert.

controls.Title: Alert Policy — Email Forwarding Rules
controls.Description: An alert policy notifies admins when new email forwarding rules are created.
controls.BusinessRisk: Forwarding rules are the primary BEC persistence mechanism. Without alerts, silent exfiltration continues indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Policies > Alert policies > Enable: Email forwarding activities.

controls.Title: Alert Policy — Unusual Mail Volume
controls.Description: An alert policy detects unusual increases in email reported as phish by users.
controls.BusinessRisk: Volume spikes indicate active phishing campaigns. Without alerts, campaigns run until users complain.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Policies > Alert policies > Enable: Unusual increase in email reported as phish.

controls.Title: Transport Rules Changes Audited
controls.Description: Organization-level audit logging captures transport rule creation and modification.
controls.BusinessRisk: Attackers create transport rules to reroute or intercept mail. Without audit logs, rule changes are invisible.
controls.Remediation: Set-OrganizationConfig -AuditDisabled $false

controls.Title: OneDrive Sync Client Restricted to Managed Devices
controls.Description: OneDrive sync is restricted to domain-joined or Intune-managed devices via tenant GUID allowlist.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unrestricted sync allows personal, unmanaged devices to mirror all SharePoint and OneDrive content locally.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -AllowedDomainGuidsForSyncApp @('your-tenant-guid')

controls.Title: External Sharing Link Expiration Configured
controls.Description: Anonymous sharing links are configured to expire after 30 days or less.
controls.BusinessRisk: Permanent anonymous links remain accessible indefinitely even after the sharing need ends.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -RequireAnonymousLinksExpireInDays 30

controls.Title: SharePoint Third-Party Apps From Store Restricted
controls.Description: Third-party app installation from the SharePoint store is disabled or restricted. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Store apps can access SharePoint data with user-granted permissions. Malicious or compromised apps exfiltrate data.
controls.Remediation: SharePoint Admin Center > Settings > Apps > disable apps from the SharePoint store.

controls.Title: Custom Script Disabled on SharePoint Sites
controls.Description: Custom script execution is disabled on personal and all SharePoint sites.
controls.BusinessRisk: Custom scripts enable JavaScript injection into SharePoint pages — XSS, credential harvesting, and data exfiltration.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOSite -DenyAddAndCustomizePages 1 (applies to all sites)

controls.Title: Third-Party Cloud Storage Disabled in SharePoint
controls.Description: Third-party storage services (Dropbox, Box, Google Drive) are disabled in SharePoint and OneDrive. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Third-party storage integration allows organizational data to move to unmanaged cloud services outside DLP policy coverage.
controls.Remediation: SharePoint Admin Center > Settings > Third-party storage services > Off

controls.Title: Email Attestation Required for Sharing
controls.Description: External users must verify their email address before accessing shared SharePoint content. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without email attestation, anonymous link recipients have no identity verification — links shared to wrong address are accessed by unintended parties.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -EmailAttestationRequired $true -EmailAttestationReAuthDays 30

controls.Title: Reauthentication Required for Sharing Links
controls.Description: Users accessing SharePoint via sharing links must reauthenticate periodically.
controls.BusinessRisk: Persistent sharing link sessions allow access after the sharing intent has ended or the account has been compromised.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -EmailAttestationReAuthDays 30

controls.Title: OneDrive Sync Restricted by Tenant Domain GUID
controls.Description: OneDrive sync is restricted to authorized tenant GUIDs preventing cross-tenant syncing. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without GUID restriction, users can sync organizational data to personal O365 tenants or competitor tenants.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -AllowedDomainGuidsForSyncApp (Get-SPOTenant).TenantId

controls.Title: Skype Consumer Contact Disabled
controls.Description: Contact from Skype consumer users is disabled in Teams federation settings.
controls.BusinessRisk: Skype consumer accounts are unverifiable and cannot be audited. They enable social engineering from identities outside any corporate identity provider.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTenantFederationConfiguration -AllowPublicUsers $false

controls.Title: Unverified App Publisher Blocked
controls.Description: Teams app permission policy restricts installation to Microsoft and verified third-party publishers only.
controls.BusinessRisk: Apps from unverified publishers have not been vetted. Malicious Teams apps can access meeting transcripts, chat, and files.
controls.Remediation: Teams Admin Center > Teams apps > Permission policies > Block all apps from unknown publishers.

controls.Title: Third-Party Cloud Storage Disabled in Teams
controls.Description: Dropbox, Box, Google Drive, and ShareFile integrations are disabled in Teams client configuration. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Third-party storage allows data to move from Teams to unmanaged cloud services outside DLP and retention policy coverage.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsClientConfiguration -AllowDropbox $false -AllowBox $false -AllowGoogleDrive $false -AllowShareFile $false

controls.Title: Teams Email Integration Disabled
controls.Description: Email into Teams channels is disabled — channel email addresses cannot receive inbound email.
controls.BusinessRisk: Channel email addresses bypass email security controls. Phishing payloads can be injected directly into Teams without going through EOP.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsClientConfiguration -AllowEmailIntoChannels $false

controls.Title: Cloud Recording Disabled for External Participants
controls.Description: External participants cannot initiate or control meeting cloud recordings. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: External participants recording meetings capture confidential discussions with no internal awareness or consent controls.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowCloudRecordingForCalls $false for externally-scoped policies

controls.Title: Broad Channel Meeting Invite Restricted
controls.Description: Channel meeting scheduling that invites all channel members is restricted or requires deliberate selection. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Channel meetings can inadvertently include all members — including external guests — in sensitive meeting invites.
controls.Remediation: Review AllowChannelMeetingScheduling in meeting policy — restrict if channels have broad guest membership.

controls.Title: External User Chat Restricted
controls.Description: External users cannot initiate unsolicited chats with internal users via Teams federation.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unrestricted external chat enables social engineering directly in Teams, bypassing email phishing controls entirely.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTenantFederationConfiguration -AllowFederatedUsers $false or restrict to allowlist.

controls.Title: PSTN Users Cannot Bypass Lobby
controls.Description: PSTN dial-in users are not permitted to bypass the Teams meeting lobby.
controls.BusinessRisk: PSTN users have no identity verification. Auto-admit to meetings allows uninvited callers to join sensitive discussions.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowPSTNUsersToBypassLobby $false

controls.Title: Purview Audit Log Search Enabled
controls.Description: Unified audit log search is enabled and accessible to compliance administrators. (No SCuBA equivalent — Purview audit/DLP covered in MS.DEFENDER.* baselines.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without audit log search, compliance teams cannot investigate incidents, fulfill legal holds, or demonstrate regulatory compliance.
controls.Remediation: Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled $true

controls.Title: Communication Compliance Policy Active
controls.Description: At least one communication compliance policy is active for regulatory monitoring. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: In regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government), unmonitored communications create compliance liability.
controls.Remediation: Purview > Communication compliance > Create policy. Requires E5 Compliance.

controls.Title: Information Barriers Mode Configured
controls.Description: Information barriers are configured in Single or Multi-segment mode if barriers are deployed. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Legacy information barriers mode has known limitations. Modern mode provides more reliable segment enforcement.
controls.Remediation: Set-PolicyConfig -InformationBarrierMode MultiSegment. Requires E5 Compliance.

controls.Title: Insider Risk Management Policy Active
controls.Description: At least one insider risk management policy is configured to detect data theft or policy violations. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Departing employees and malicious insiders can exfiltrate significant data undetected without insider risk monitoring.
controls.Remediation: Purview > Insider risk management > Create policy (Departing user data theft, Data leaks). Requires E5 Compliance.

controls.Title: Retention Policy Covers Key Workloads
controls.Description: Retention policies are configured for Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without retention policies, data subject to legal hold cannot be preserved. Data may be deleted before regulatory retention requirements are met.
controls.Remediation: Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies. Create policies for Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams.

controls.Title: Auto-Labeling Policy Active
controls.Description: At least one auto-labeling policy automatically applies sensitivity labels to content without user action.
controls.BusinessRisk: Manual labeling is inconsistent. Sensitive data without labels is not protected by policies that depend on classification.
controls.Remediation: Purview > Information protection > Auto-labeling > Create policy for sensitive info types (SSN, credit card, etc.).

controls.Title: Endpoint Detection and Response Policy Deployed
controls.Description: MDE onboarding policy is deployed via Intune, enrolling endpoints into Defender for Endpoint. (No SCuBA equivalent — Intune device-compliance controls are out of SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without EDR, endpoint threats are invisible. Lateral movement, persistence, and data theft go undetected.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Endpoint security > Endpoint detection and response > Create policy for Windows.

controls.Title: Attack Surface Reduction Rules Enabled
controls.Description: ASR rules are deployed via Intune endpoint security policy to block common malware delivery techniques. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: ASR rules block Office macro abuse, credential theft from LSASS, and script-based attacks. Without them, commodity malware delivery succeeds.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Endpoint security > Attack surface reduction > Create ASR rules policy. Enable at minimum: Block Office macros, Block credential stealing, Block JS/VBS launching executables.

controls.Title: Windows Firewall Policy Deployed via Intune
controls.Description: Windows Defender Firewall is managed and enforced via Intune endpoint security policy. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without managed firewall policy, users can disable the firewall or create rules that expose services to lateral movement.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Endpoint security > Firewall > Create Windows Firewall policy. Enable domain, private, and public profiles.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: macOS FileVault Encryption Required
controls.Description: macOS compliance policy requires FileVault disk encryption. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Stolen or lost Macs without FileVault expose all organizational data stored locally, including cached credentials and documents.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Devices > Compliance > macOS > Create policy > System Security > Require encryption = Require.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Windows Update Compliance Policy Deployed
controls.Description: Windows Update for Business rings or compliance policy ensures devices receive security updates on schedule. (No SCuBA equivalent — Intune device-compliance controls are out of SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Unpatched endpoints are the most exploited attack surface. Without managed update policy, critical patches may sit unapplied for months.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Devices > Windows update rings > Create update ring with feature and quality update schedules.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: PIM Alerts Configured
controls.Description: PIM security alerts are configured to detect stale, redundant, and outside-PIM role assignments.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without PIM alerts, over-privileged accounts, dormant admins, and bypass assignments go undetected indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Alerts > Review and enable: Roles assigned outside PIM, Redundant assignments, Stale assignments, Too many GA admins.

controls.Title: Access Reviews for Privileged Roles
controls.Description: Recurring access reviews are configured for privileged directory roles via PIM.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without access reviews, stale privileged accounts accumulate over time, increasing attack surface with every employee departure or role change.
controls.Remediation: PIM > Azure AD roles > Access reviews > Create recurring quarterly review for Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Security Admin.

controls.Title: Authenticator Number Matching Enabled
controls.Description: Microsoft Authenticator number matching is explicitly enabled to prevent MFA fatigue attacks.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without number matching, attackers can spam MFA push requests until a user accidentally approves. MFA fatigue is a documented breach technique.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Security > Authentication methods > Microsoft Authenticator > Configure > Require number matching = Enabled.

controls.Title: Passwordless Authentication Methods Available
controls.Description: FIDO2 security keys or Windows Hello for Business are enabled as passwordless authentication options.
controls.BusinessRisk: Password-based auth carries inherent credential theft risk. Passwordless methods eliminate the credential as an attack vector entirely.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Security > Authentication methods > FIDO2 security key > Enable. Consider Windows Hello for Business via Intune policy.

controls.Title: Identity Protection Risky User Workflow
controls.Description: Automated response to Identity Protection risky user signals via CA user risk policy.
controls.BusinessRisk: Identity Protection detects credential compromise, leaked credentials, and anomalous sign-ins. Without automated response, the human follow-up is too slow.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Condition = User risk = High, Grant = Require password change. Requires Entra P2.

controls.Title: Privileged Accounts Use Dedicated Cloud-Only Accounts
controls.Description: All privileged role holders use separate cloud-only accounts, not their day-to-day synced accounts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Using the same account for email and admin work means email phishing can compromise admin credentials. Dedicated admin accounts separate the blast radius.
controls.Remediation: Create dedicated .admin or .onmicrosoft.com accounts for all privileged role holders. Remove privileged roles from on-premises synced accounts.

controls.Title: Break-Glass Account Sign-In Monitoring
controls.Description: Alerts are configured to notify immediately if break-glass accounts are used.
controls.BusinessRisk: Break-glass accounts bypass all CA policies. Any use should be treated as an incident — either a genuine emergency or unauthorized access.
controls.Remediation: Create a Sentinel or Defender XDR alert rule for sign-in from break-glass account UPNs. Notify SOC immediately on any sign-in.

controls.Title: Sign-in Frequency Session Control Configured
controls.Description: CA policy enforces sign-in frequency to limit the lifetime of stolen session tokens. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Stolen tokens (from AiTM, token theft via malware) remain valid for the full session lifetime. Sign-in frequency limits re-use to the configured window.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Session controls > Sign-in frequency > set to 1 hour for privileged users, 8 hours for standard users.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Mailbox Audit Log Age Limit Sufficient
controls.Description: Mailbox audit log retention is set to at least 90 days (180 days recommended).
controls.BusinessRisk: Short audit retention prevents investigation of incidents discovered after the retention window closes. 90 days is the absolute minimum for most IR scenarios.
controls.Remediation: Set-Mailbox -Identity All -AuditLogAgeLimit 180

controls.Title: Admin Audit Log Enabled
controls.Description: Exchange admin audit logging captures all cmdlet execution by administrators.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without admin audit logs, malicious or accidental admin changes (transport rules, forwarding, policy changes) are not recorded.
controls.Remediation: Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -AdminAuditLogEnabled $true

controls.Title: Safe Attachments for SharePoint OneDrive Teams Enabled
controls.Description: Safe Attachments ATP protection is enabled globally for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams file scanning.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without this setting, malicious files uploaded to SharePoint/OD/Teams are not sandboxed. Files can be shared across the tenant before detection.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Policies > Safe Attachments > Global settings > Enable Defender for Office 365 for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams.

controls.Title: Anti-Spam Inbound Policy Properly Configured
controls.Description: Default inbound anti-spam policy has appropriate action settings for spam, phish, and bulk mail.
controls.BusinessRisk: Misconfigured inbound anti-spam allows higher volumes of spam and phishing to reach user inboxes.
controls.Remediation: Set-HostedContentFilterPolicy -Identity Default -SpamAction MoveToJmf -BulkThreshold 6 -ZapEnabled $true

controls.Title: Unauthenticated Sender Indicator Enabled
controls.Description: Anti-phishing policy shows a ? indicator in Outlook when sender cannot be authenticated via SPF or DKIM.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without the unauth indicator, users cannot visually distinguish spoofed from legitimate email in Outlook. No visual friction for display name attacks.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -EnableUnauthenticatedSender $true

controls.Title: Via Tag Enabled in Anti-Phishing Policy
controls.Description: Anti-phishing policy shows the via tag when a sender uses a relay service.
controls.BusinessRisk: Relay-based spoofing is harder to detect without the via tag — attackers use legitimate relays to send spoofed email that passes SPF.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy -Identity Default -EnableViaTag $true

controls.Title: Defender for Cloud Apps Connected
controls.Description: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps M365 connector is active for shadow IT discovery and session controls. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without MDCA, OAuth app abuse, shadow IT cloud usage, and anomalous user behavior in cloud apps goes undetected.
controls.Remediation: Defender XDR > Settings > Cloud Apps > Connected apps > Office 365 > Connect.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (M365 E5 or add-on)

controls.Title: Defender Alert Email Notifications Configured
controls.Description: Security team receives email notifications for high and critical Defender alerts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without email notification, Defender alerts only surface if someone actively checks the portal. Critical incidents go undetected until manual review.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Settings > Email notifications > Create notification rule for High and Critical severity alerts.

controls.Title: Site Collection Admin Access Reviewed
controls.Description: Site collection administrators are reviewed periodically to ensure no excessive accumulation. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Site collection admins have full read/write access to all site content. Stale or excessive site admins are a data exfiltration risk.
controls.Remediation: Review site admins: Get-SPOSite -Limit ALL | ForEach-Object { Get-SPOUser -Site $_.Url -Group 'Site Collection Administrators' }

controls.Title: SharePoint Sharing Notifications Enabled
controls.Description: Content owners receive notifications when their files or folders are re-shared. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Silent re-sharing allows data to spread beyond intended recipients without the owner's awareness.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -NotifyOwnersWhenItemsReshared $true

controls.Title: OneDrive Version History Enabled
controls.Description: Version history is enabled in OneDrive and SharePoint for ransomware recovery capability. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without version history, ransomware-encrypted files cannot be recovered to a clean state. Version history is the primary ransomware recovery control for cloud storage.
controls.Remediation: Verify via SharePoint Admin Center > Settings that versioning is enabled and version limits are adequate (≥100 versions).

controls.Title: SharePoint Guest Access Expiration Required
controls.Description: External guest access to SharePoint requires periodic re-authentication and is configured to expire.
controls.BusinessRisk: Permanent guest access accumulates over time — departed vendors and ex-clients retain access to organizational data indefinitely.
controls.Remediation: Set-SPOTenant -ExternalUserExpirationRequired $true -ExternalUserExpireInDays 60

controls.Title: Teams Meeting Watermarks Enabled
controls.Description: Content watermarking is enabled for Teams meetings to deter and trace content leaks. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Meeting content shared without watermarks cannot be traced if leaked. Watermarks deter deliberate leaks and enable forensic attribution.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowWatermarkForScreenSharing $true

controls.Title: Auto-Admit Only Authenticated Organization Users
controls.Description: Teams meetings auto-admit only authenticated internal users — all others wait in lobby.
controls.BusinessRisk: Auto-admitting Everyone or All Federated allows uninvited external participants to join internal meetings without any approval.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AutoAdmittedUsers EveryoneInCompanyExcludingGuests

controls.Title: Meeting Chat Disabled for Anonymous Participants
controls.Description: Anonymous meeting participants cannot use meeting chat (EnabledExceptAnonymous or Disabled). (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Anonymous participants with chat access can send phishing links and malicious content to all authenticated meeting participants.
controls.Remediation: Set-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy -AllowMeetingChat EnabledExceptAnonymous

controls.Title: Meeting Chat Copy Prevention via DLP
controls.Description: DLP policies prevent copying sensitive meeting chat content to unauthorized locations. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Meeting chat containing sensitive discussions can be copied to personal notes, email, or external storage without DLP controls.
controls.Remediation: Purview > DLP > Create policy targeting Teams with sensitive info types to restrict copy/paste of protected content.

controls.Title: Audit Logs Exported to SIEM
controls.Description: Unified audit logs are forwarded to a SIEM (Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk, etc.) for long-term retention and correlation.
controls.BusinessRisk: Portal-only audit log access is limited to 90-180 days and requires manual querying. SIEM export enables long-term retention, correlation, and automated alerting.
controls.Remediation: Connect Microsoft Sentinel M365 data connector, or use Defender XDR > Settings > Microsoft Defender XDR > Audit log export.

controls.Title: eDiscovery Case Management Configured
controls.Description: eDiscovery roles are assigned and cases can be opened for legal hold and content search. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without eDiscovery access, compliance and legal teams cannot place holds or export content for litigation, regulatory audits, or HR investigations.
controls.Remediation: Purview > Roles & scopes > eDiscovery Manager. Assign eDiscovery Manager role to compliance team members.

controls.Title: Compliance Score Improvement Actions Tracked
controls.Description: Microsoft Purview Compliance Manager improvement actions are reviewed and assigned to owners. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Compliance Manager surfaces specific misconfiguration risks with remediation steps. Unreviewed action items represent documented known gaps.
controls.Remediation: compliance.microsoft.com > Compliance Manager > Improvement actions > Assign to owners and set target dates.

controls.Title: Sensitive Information Types Used in DLP Policies
controls.Description: DLP policies are configured with Microsoft or custom sensitive information types for automatic detection. (No SCuBA equivalent — Purview audit/DLP covered in MS.DEFENDER.* baselines.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without SIT-based detection, DLP policies provide no classification and cannot automatically protect sensitive data in transit.
controls.Remediation: Purview > DLP > Create or edit policies > Add conditions using sensitive information types (SSN, credit card, health info, etc.).

controls.Title: Device Enrollment Restrictions Configured
controls.Description: Intune enrollment restrictions limit which platforms, ownership types, and OS versions can enroll. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without enrollment restrictions, any device — including unmanaged personal or obsolete devices — can enroll and receive corporate app access.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Devices > Enrollment > Enrollment restrictions > Create policy restricting to managed platforms and minimum OS versions.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Mobile App Configuration Policies Deployed
controls.Description: App configuration policies push security settings to managed mobile apps. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without app config policies, managed apps use default settings which may not meet security baselines. Outlook, Teams, and Edge on mobile all have security-relevant configuration options.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Apps > App configuration policies > Create policy for iOS/Android targeting Outlook, Teams, Edge.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Conditional Launch Policies Configured
controls.Description: App protection policies include conditional launch settings to block jailbroken devices and enforce OS version requirements. (No SCuBA equivalent — Intune device-compliance controls are out of SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Jailbroken and rooted devices bypass OS security controls. Without conditional launch, compromised devices access corporate apps with full permissions.
controls.Remediation: Intune > Apps > App protection policies > Conditional launch > Add: Jailbroken/rooted devices = Block, Min OS version = current-1.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Intune Plan 1

controls.Title: Power Platform Connector Classification Reviewed
controls.Description: Connectors in Power Platform DLP policies are classified as Business, Non-Business, or Blocked.
controls.BusinessRisk: Unclassified connectors default to Non-Business — flows can still mix data between business connectors and unclassified ones.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Data policies > Edit existing policy > Review all connectors and assign to Business or Blocked groups.

controls.Title: Power Automate Governance Policy Active
controls.Description: Guest users are restricted from creating Power Automate flows.
controls.BusinessRisk: Guest users creating flows can automate data movement between organizational data sources and external destinations without IT oversight.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Tenant settings > Power Automate > Disable for guests.

controls.Title: Power Apps Portal Creation Restricted
controls.Description: Non-admin users are restricted from creating public-facing Power Apps portals.
controls.BusinessRisk: Power Apps portals are internet-accessible by default. Non-admin portal creation can inadvertently expose organizational data to the internet.
controls.Remediation: Power Platform Admin Center > Settings > Power Apps > Disable portal creation by non-admins.

controls.Title: Device Code Authentication Flow Blocked
controls.Description: A Conditional Access policy blocks the device code authentication flow for all users.
controls.BusinessRisk: Device code phishing attacks send victims to a legitimate Microsoft URL where they enter an attacker-controlled code — no password required. The attack is especially effective against less technical users.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Conditions > Authentication flows > Device code flow, Grant = Block. Applies to all users. Reference: MS.AAD.3.9v1 (SCuBA).
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: No Guest Accounts in Highly Privileged Roles
controls.Description: No guest accounts hold Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator, or other highly privileged directory roles.
controls.BusinessRisk: Guest accounts are outside the tenant's identity governance. A compromised guest account holding a privileged role provides tenant-wide administrative access from an uncontrolled identity.
controls.Remediation: Remove guest accounts from all privileged roles. Use cloud-only member accounts for all administrative functions. Reference: MS.AAD.7.7v1 (SCuBA).

controls.Title: Risky Service Principals Reviewed and Remediated
controls.Description: No service principals are flagged as risky by Entra ID Identity Protection without active remediation.
controls.BusinessRisk: Risky service principals represent compromised application identities with persistent, non-interactive access to all assigned resource scopes. Unlike user accounts, they have no MFA and no session expiry.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Identity Protection > Risky workload identities. Investigate and remediate flagged service principals. Disable or rotate credentials for compromised identities. Requires Entra ID P2 Workload Identities.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Entra ID P2 + Workload Identities add-on

controls.Title: Token Protection (Session Binding) Enforced
controls.Description: CA policy enforces token protection to bind tokens to the originating device, blocking replay attacks. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: AiTM (adversary-in-the-middle) phishing steals authenticated session tokens. Without token binding, stolen tokens work from any device — effectively bypassing MFA entirely.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Session controls > Token protection. Enable for Exchange Online and SharePoint Online at minimum. Requires Entra ID P1+. Note: may cause issues with non-compliant clients — test in report-only mode first.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Continuous Access Evaluation Strict Mode
controls.Description: CA policy enforces CAE strict mode ensuring near-real-time session revocation when risk or network conditions change. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Default CAE uses best-effort revocation with up to 60-minute delays. Strict mode provides near-real-time enforcement when IP changes or risk signals fire.
controls.Remediation: CA policy > Session controls > Customize continuous access evaluation > Strict enforcement.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Cross-Tenant Inbound Trust Settings Restricted
controls.Description: Default cross-tenant access settings do not trust external MFA or device compliance claims from unknown tenants.
controls.BusinessRisk: Trusting MFA from all external tenants means an attacker controlling a weak tenant can satisfy your MFA requirements using their own weaker authentication — effectively bypassing your Conditional Access policies.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > External Identities > Cross-tenant access settings > Default settings > Inbound trust. Disable 'Trust MFA from Microsoft Entra tenants' and 'Trust compliant devices' unless specific B2B partner trust is required.

controls.Title: Privileged Access Workstation or Device Scope for Admins
controls.Description: Conditional Access enforces that privileged role activations or admin operations occur from compliant or specifically designated devices.
controls.BusinessRisk: Admins authenticating from any arbitrary device — including personal laptops with no endpoint controls — expose privileged credentials to malware on unmanaged endpoints.
controls.Remediation: CA policy: Target admin roles > Require compliant device or Hybrid AD join. Alternatively define a named device group as a PAW group and require membership.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1 + Intune

controls.Title: Terms of Use Policy Enforced
controls.Description: A CA policy enforces Terms of Use acceptance for users accessing organizational resources. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Without ToU enforcement, there is no documented acknowledgment of acceptable use policy — creating legal and HR gaps, especially for guest users and contractors.
controls.Remediation: Entra ID > Security > Conditional Access > Terms of use > Create ToU document. Add to CA policy: Grant > Require terms of use.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 Business Premium or Entra ID P1

controls.Title: Workload Identity Conditional Access Policy
controls.Description: CA policies are applied to service principals and managed identities, not just user accounts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without CA policies on workload identities, compromised service principals face no access controls — no location restriction, no risk-based blocking, no session limits.
controls.Remediation: CA policy > Users > Select Workload identities > Apply conditions and access controls to high-privilege service principals. Requires Entra Workload Identities Premium add-on.

controls.Title: DLP Policy Covers All Key Workloads
controls.Description: DLP policies are active for Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams simultaneously.
controls.BusinessRisk: DLP covering only email allows the same sensitive data to be shared freely via Teams or uploaded to SharePoint. Attackers and departing employees exploit uncovered channels.
controls.Remediation: Purview > DLP > Create or edit policy > Choose where to apply > Enable Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, Devices simultaneously. Reference: MS.DEFENDER.4.1v2 (SCuBA).

controls.Title: DLP Policy Uses Sensitive Information Types
controls.Description: At least one DLP policy uses sensitive information types (SSN, credit card, health data) for automatic content detection.
controls.BusinessRisk: DLP without SITs cannot automatically identify regulated or sensitive content. Policy enforcement is entirely manual and depends on users correctly classifying their own files.
controls.Remediation: Purview > DLP > Edit policy > Add condition > Content contains > Sensitive info types. Reference: MS.DEFENDER.4.2v1 (SCuBA).

controls.Title: Risky OAuth Application Alerts Configured
controls.Description: Alerts are configured for high-privilege OAuth consent grants and risky application behavior.
controls.BusinessRisk: Consent phishing grants attacker-controlled apps persistent access. Without alerts, malicious OAuth grants go undetected until the app is used for data theft.
controls.Remediation: Defender XDR > Cloud Apps > Policies > OAuth app policies. Create policy: App = apps with high privileges, consented by users. Requires Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps. Reference: MS.DEFENDER.5.1v1 (SCuBA).
controls.LicenseRequirement: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (M365 E5 or add-on)

controls.Title: Priority Accounts Tagged in Defender
controls.Description: Executives and IT admins are tagged as priority accounts in Defender for Office 365 for enhanced protection and differentiated alerts.
controls.BusinessRisk: Priority accounts are disproportionately targeted by BEC, spear phishing, and executive impersonation. Untagged, they receive the same protection as any general user account.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Settings > Email & collaboration > User tags > Global Settings > Priority account protection. Tag executives and admins as priority accounts.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 2 (M365 E5 or add-on)

controls.Title: Endpoint DLP Policy Active on Managed Devices
controls.Description: Endpoint DLP policies monitor or block sensitive data actions on Windows endpoints — copy to USB, upload to unsanctioned sites, print.
controls.BusinessRisk: Without Endpoint DLP, a user can copy an entire SharePoint document library to a USB drive or personal Google Drive without any policy enforcement — no alert, no block, no log.
controls.Remediation: Purview > DLP > Create policy > Devices location > Enable. Requires Defender for Endpoint onboarding + Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft 365 E5 Compliance.
controls.LicenseRequirement: M365 E5 or E5 Compliance add-on

controls.Title: Attack Simulation Training Campaigns Active
controls.Description: Regular phishing simulation campaigns are run via Attack Simulation Training in Defender. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
controls.BusinessRisk: Human error causes >90% of breaches. Without regular simulation, users have no trained muscle memory for identifying phishing — click-through rates on real attacks remain high.
controls.Remediation: Defender portal > Email & collaboration > Attack simulation training > Simulations > Launch simulation. Schedule recurring simulations covering credential harvest, link in attachment, drive-by URL techniques. Requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 2.
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 2 (M365 E5 or add-on)

controls.Title: Safe Links Protects Office Applications
controls.Description: Safe Links policy is configured to scan URLs in Office documents (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and Teams messages at click time.
controls.BusinessRisk: Malicious URLs embedded in Office files are not scanned by default Safe Links email policies. A user receiving a document containing a malicious link via email attachment can click it without Safe Links intervening.
controls.Remediation: Set-SafeLinksPolicy -EnableSafeLinksForO365 $true. Or via Defender portal > Policies > Safe links > Enable Office 365 apps protection. Reference: MS.DEFENDER.2.1v1 (SCuBA).
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 (M365 Business Premium)

controls.Title: Per-User Mailbox Audit Logging Enabled for All Mailboxes
controls.Description: AuditEnabled is set to $true on all mailboxes — no mailboxes have audit logging explicitly disabled.
controls.BusinessRisk: Mailboxes without audit logging cannot be investigated after a BEC incident. Inbox rules, delegation grants, and mail access are not recorded for unaudited mailboxes.
controls.Remediation: Get-Mailbox -ResultSize Unlimited | Where-Object { $_.AuditEnabled -eq $false } | Set-Mailbox -AuditEnabled $true. Reference: MS.EXO.4.1v1 (SCuBA).

controls.Title: Priority Account Protection Active in Anti-Phishing Policy
controls.Description: Tagged priority accounts are explicitly protected by targeted user impersonation protection in Defender anti-phishing policy.
controls.BusinessRisk: Priority accounts are the highest-value BEC targets. Without explicit impersonation protection, spoofed executive emails are handled the same as general spam.
controls.Remediation: Set-AntiPhishPolicy > TargetedUsersToProtect — add executive UPNs. Enable EnableTargetedUserProtection. Reference: MS.DEFENDER.1.6v1 (SCuBA).
controls.LicenseRequirement: Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 (M365 Business Premium)

controls.Title: No Allowed Sender Domains Bypassing Anti-Spam Filtering
controls.Description: The anti-spam policy contains no allowed sender domains that would cause all mail from those domains to bypass EOP filtering. (No SCuBA equivalent — NLS/CIS hardening beyond SCuBA scope.)
